title: No Choice
tagline: This is where you find what you're made of.
hook_line: When retreat means failure and advance means destruction — that is where you find what you are made of.

scenes.title: No Options Left
scenes.narration: When there is no option left — when retreat means failure and advance means destruction — that is where you find what you are built from.
scenes.visual_prompt: Epic wide shot, all six mechs in battle formation facing the full advancing Persian army, scale incomprehensible, the army filling the entire frame behind them, six tiny lights — orange, slate blue, cyan, purple, ancient bronze, crimson — against two and a half million, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: BLAZE — First In
scenes.narration: BLAZE hit first. He always hit first. The impact was immediate and enormous. He didn't stop. First in, last to stop.
scenes.visual_prompt: BLAZE orange mech launching forward into Persian formation, amber energy core blazing at full power, the point of impact sending shockwaves outward, warriors scattering, but more immediately closing behind him, action blur and energy, never stopping, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: GRANITE Holds Both Flanks
scenes.narration: GRANITE held both flanks simultaneously. The cryo-sleeve at limit. His geological armor taking hits that had destroyed previous mechs. He didn't stop.
scenes.visual_prompt: GRANITE slate blue-grey 12 meters, both arms extended to cover flanking attacks simultaneously, cryo-sleeve blazing frost at maximum, repaired crack visible and glowing, geological strata armor showing deep impact marks, holding against force from two sides at once, cinematic action, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: NOVA — Seven Panels
scenes.narration: NOVA had seven panels active — the most since the jamming. She routed strike coordinates to BLAZE and RUMBLE simultaneously. Her systems were at the edge. She didn't stop.
scenes.visual_prompt: NOVA cold cyan blue mech, seven holographic panels active in combat mode, routing strike data to multiple team members simultaneously, panels flickering under jamming pressure but holding, cyan light intense and focused, NOVA's posture showing system strain but absolute resolve, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: STORM — Everywhere
scenes.narration: STORM moved at speeds the sensors couldn't track. Eleven precision strikes in six seconds. She was the only one nobody could see at all.
scenes.visual_prompt: Multiple exposure composite showing STORM at eleven different position simultaneously, electric purple motion blur streaks connecting them, each position showing a precision strike moment, the total pattern forming something beautiful and devastating, impossibly fast, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: RUMBLE's Decision
scenes.narration: RUMBLE made a decision nobody asked him to make. He advanced. Into the army. Alone. It was tactically insane. It bought them forty seconds.
scenes.visual_prompt: RUMBLE crimson red and black 28 meters charging forward alone DEEPER into the Persian army — against the direction of retreat — war hammer deployed, massive impacts, units scattering in all directions from his path, from above we can see him creating a distraction vortex that draws force away from the team, cinematic epic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: The Kind of Hit That Ended SHADOW-1
scenes.narration: BLAZE took a hit. The archive showed SHADOW-1 had been destroyed by a hit exactly like it. BLAZE got up.
scenes.visual_prompt: BLAZE hot orange mech knocked to the ground by a massive Persian force impact, amber energy core flickering, one frame of him down and still, then the core blazes back to full and he is getting up — not repaired, just unwilling, armor cracked and scarred, cinematic ground-level impact, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: Pushed Back
scenes.narration: They were being pushed back. Step by step. The army was too large. But they didn't break. Because breaking wasn't a choice they were willing to make.
scenes.visual_prompt: Wide shot, the six mechs maintaining formation while being pushed backward, each one fighting, none breaking formation or retreating independently, the army pressing them from every direction, each mech in their role — GRANITE flanks, BLAZE front, NOVA coordinating, STORM striking, RUMBLE forcing through — cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: He Makes No Notation
scenes.narration: Xerxes watched. He made no notation. He didn't need to. He already had everything he needed to know.
scenes.visual_prompt: Xerxes commanding presence, looking toward the battle, his notation implement still — not writing — the absence of the notation more significant than any previous notation, absolute certainty in his posture, he is done studying, the battle visible in the distance, cinematic wide, 1080x1920 vertical

scenes.title: Twice This
scenes.narration: ELDER-7, as the advance finally pulled back for the night: 'Tomorrow he sends twice this.' Silence across the team. Then BLAZE: 'Good.'
scenes.visual_prompt: The team battered and scarred in the aftermath, ELDER-7 data pad updating, each mech showing combat damage — GRANITE's cryo-sleeve crack wider, NOVA's panels at four, BLAZE's armor cracked, RUMBLE's war hammer dented — and then BLAZE small and orange in the foreground with absolute certainty in his stance, cinematic, 1080x1920 vertical

viral_hook: Tomorrow he sends twice this. BLAZE's response: 'Good.'
highlight_note: BLAZE's single word 'Good.' — after everything they've been through, the damage they've taken, what they know is coming — is the character-defining moment of the season.
rewatch_bait: Xerxes making no notation in scene 9. After watching him notate every other episode, the absence hits differently. He's done learning. He already knows what he'll do.
youtube_title: Tomorrow He Sends Twice as Many. BLAZE's Response: 'Good.' | Mech Legends S1 EP009
lesson: There is a point beyond strategy where all you have left is who you are. That's the most honest version of anyone.
arc_notes: EP009 is maximum pressure before the revelation. Every member at their limit. RUMBLE's solo charge into the army is his defining moment — he proved himself by doing what no one asked. BLAZE's 'Good.' sets up EP010 with perfect contrast — the lowest point followed by the insight.
